Chatmail
HomeClientsRelays

How to select a chatmail relay?

Chatmail relays are each operated by different groups and people. Visit a chatmail relay link and then tap the QR invite code there to create a chatmail address in your favorite chatmail app:

  • nine.relay-a.example is the default onboarding relay for many chatmail apps

  • relay-b.example is geared towards German speaking users

  • relay-c.example is geared towards Polish speaking users

  • chatmail.relay-d.example and chatmail.relay-e.example are focused on Italian speaking users

  • chat.relay-f.example is hosted in Falkenstein, Germany and is geared towards German speaking users

  • chika.deeply.nested.example is hosted in Santa Clara, California US

  • relay-g.example is hosted in Nuremberg, Germany

  • d.relay-h.example is hosted in Roubaix, France

  • relay-i.example is hosted in Quebec, Canada

  • relay-j.example is hosted in Melbourne, Australia

  • e2ee-k.example is hosted in Johannesburg, South Africa

  • chat.relay-l.example is hosted in Bangalore, India

  • e2ee-m.example is hosted in Orastie, Romania

  • relay-n.example is hosted in Warsaw, Poland

  • relay-o.example is hosted in Helsinki, Finland

  • chat.in-the-p.example is hosted in Falkenstein, Germany

  • chat.relay-q.example is hosted in Prague, Czechia

  • relay-r.example is hosted in Zug, Switzerland

  • chat.relay-s.example is hosted in Lviv, Ukraine

  • chat.relay-t.example is hosted in Iceland, Japan, Kenya, and South Africa

  • delta.relay-u.example is hosted in Gravelines, France

  • chat.relay-v.example is hosted in Frankfurt, Germany

  • relay-w.example is hosted in Elk Grove Village, Illinois US

  • relay-x.example is hosted in Ashburn, Virginia US

  • delta.relay-y.example is hosted in Roon, Netherlands

Several additional chatmail relays are not listed publically because some countries tried to block published relays, and also because some operators prefer to do their own outreach to communities of their choice. Private or public, chatmail relays are always interoperable with each other.

Welcome to decentralized messaging :)

For further background on chatmail relays, and how to setup one yourself, please refer to the https://chatmail.at/doc/relay documentation.
